headline: Delta’s Higher Fares And Shelf Offering Could Be A Game Changer For Delta Air Lines (DAL)
summary: In July 2026, Delta Air Lines reported second-quarter revenue of US$19,757 million, up from US$16,648 million a year earlier, while net income declined to US$1,604 million from US$2,130 million, and it also filed an omnibus shelf registration covering common and preferred stock, debt, warrants, rights, purchase contracts, and units. Management emphasized that higher airfares reflect a reset in pricing rather than temporary fuel surcharges, suggesting that Delta’s growing focus on premium...

headline: Major airline makes it easier to get refunds for Middle East flights
summary: International airlines are gradually restarting some flights to the Middle East, but the volatility set off by the U.S. strike on Iran continues to make establishing any kind of timeframe challenging. headline: Delta (DAL) Is Resetting Fares Higher As Premium Travel Demand Holds Up
summary: Delta Air Lines is raising fares to offset higher fuel costs linked to recent geopolitical events. The company is keeping fares at a higher baseline even if fuel prices ease, citing reduced discounting across the industry. Delta is leaning on growing demand for premium and corporate travel, with new premium products and lounges supporting this shift. Delta Air Lines, traded as NYSE:DAL, is reshaping its pricing approach at a time when its stock has risen 24.5% year to date and 57.0% over...
